articles.title: Nifty could slip towards 23,150 if key support breaks: Rupak De
articles.description: Indian equity markets ended last week under pressure, with benchmark indices struggling to hold gains due to weak technical indicators and selling in heavyweight stocks. Analyst Rupak De notes a fragile technical structure for Nifty and Bank Nifty, anticipating further downside. While smallcaps corrected sharply, De sees selective buying opportunities emerging in mid and smallcap segments.

articles.title: India's top IT firms pay record Rs 1 lakh crore dividend even as stocks take a beating
articles.description: TCS, the index's largest constituent, saw its dividend decline 12.7 percent to Rs 39,820 crore in FY26 from Rs 45,588 crore in FY25. Coforge recorded a steeper fall, with its combined payout dropping over 20 percent to Rs 404 crore from Rs 509 crore in the previous fiscal. HCL Technology held its dividend unchanged at Rs 16,290 crore.

articles.title: World Hypertension Day: Bhagwant Mann government focuses on prevention, timely detection and affordable treatment for hypertension
articles.description: Hypertension, commonly known as high blood pressure, is often described by doctors as the “silent killer”. It shows little warning before triggering strokes, heart failure or kidney disease. The condition Is common across age groups. Amid this growing health crisis, Punjab’s Mukh Mantri Sehat Yojna has become a safety net.

articles.title: MrBeast fires back after viral podcast claim says his YouTube channel views suddenly dropped by 50 percent
articles.description: MrBeast responded after online claims said his YouTube views dropped by 50%. He explained that people were comparing years-old videos with newer uploads. The creator said his older videos continue gaining millions of views every month. Despite the debate, MrBeast remains YouTube’s most-followed individual creator, and his recent videos still attract tens of millions of viewers quickly.

articles.title: Zelensky fails to strike targets in Russia after Putin's 1400 drone barrage: Kyiv using faulty UAVs?
articles.description: Russia said on May 16 that a Ukrainian drone attacked the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant site and fell near the power units without detonating, according to a statement carried by TASS. Russia also said on May 17 that air defenses repelled another drone attack on Moscow, with Mayor Sergey Sobyanin saying ten more UAVs were shot down, while the Russian Defense Ministry claimed its forces captured Borovaya and Kutkovka in the Kharkiv region.

Fishermen are advised to avoid the sea, and travellers should anticipate potential disruptions in popular tourist spots.
